Dogfoods this repo's own `CLAUDE.md` (R14.AC1). C5 lab-traced; areas 7,8,26,28 traced; `make check` green. Consumes unverified `context-cmds`/`output-styles` (L1).
      - [x] **U5** `05-ship-a-feature` (C6, W1) — flagship Daily-Driver loop **explore→plan→code→commit**. `unit.md` references the generalized W1 in `meta/workflows.md` (not re-explained, R5.AC5); teaches plan mode as a review gate (area 9) + extended-thinking awareness (area 10, mention+pointer); two CV gates (the plan, then the diff). **Build-a-feature lab** `u05-lab1` (write-path, like U1): ship `GET /projects/{id}/stats` (per-status counts, zero-filled, ownership-404). Git refs `start/u05-lab1` (tag) + `solution/u05-lab1` (branch: `ProjectStats` schema + `project_task_stats` service via `get_project` + thin route + 3 tests); objective `verify.sh` checks contract + ownership + suite-green against the learner's working tree; `reset-lab`/`verify-lab` verified end-to-end. C6 lab-traced; areas 9,10 traced; `make check` green. Consumes verified `plan-mode`/`thinking` vd keys. Woven CV/security edge (foreign-project 404) satisfies R10.AC7 for this workflow lab.
      - [x] **U6** `06-tdd` (C7, W2) — Daily-Driver **red→green** test-first loop. `unit.md` references generalized W2 in `meta/workflows.md`; teaches writing the test first, **confirming red for the right reason** (assertion, not import/typo), implementing to green, and **reading the impl** (a test can be satisfied the wrong way) — the CV gate for test-driven work. **Test-first lab** `u06-lab1` (write-path): add an `overdue` filter to `GET /tasks` (overdue iff `due_date` strictly before today **and** `status != done`; done/due-today/no-due-date excluded; absent param unchanged). Git refs `start/u06-lab1` (tag) + `solution/u06-lab1` (branch: `TaskFilters.overdue` + predicate in `list_tasks` vs `date.today()` + one route query param + edge-case tests); objective `verify.sh` checks contract + no-regression suite-green against the learner's working tree; `verify-lab` verified end-to-end (fails clean = all 6 returned, the right-reason red; passes on solution). C7 lab-traced; area 11 traced; `make check` green. Consumes `test-run` vd key (kept `unverified` under L1). Edge-case assertions are the woven CV (R10.AC7).
      - [x] **U7** `07-debug-a-failure` (C8, W3) — Daily-Driver **scientific-method debugging**: reproduce → capture a re-runnable repro → **confirm root cause** (resist the AI's plausible symptom-patch) → fix → no-regress. `unit.md` references generalized W3 in `meta/workflows.md`. **Debug lab** `u07-lab1` on the **legacy** `taskflow-cli`: fix baked-in bug **D1** (overdue never flags — ISO date compared against `MM-DD-YYYY` string, copy-pasted in 3 sites) at its root. Git refs `start/u07-lab1` (tag = legacy with bug present) + `solution/u07-lab1` (branch: correct `is_overdue` via `date.fromisoformat`, `fmt_due`/`print_task_full` routed through it). Objective `verify.sh` drives the CLI via subprocess over a throwaway DB (legacy has no pytest): past-due flagged across `list --overdue`/`stats`/`list`+`show` display, future/no-due/done excluded, basic CLI intact; verified end-to-end (fails on start = the automated repro, passes on solution). C8 lab-traced; area 12 traced; `make check` green. No version-specific keys (no L1 debt). D2/D3 left for extra practice + U9.

Git refs `start/u05-lab1` (tag) + `solution/u05-lab1` (branch: `ProjectStats` schema + `project_task_stats` service via `get_project` + thin route + 3 tests); objective `verify.sh` checks contract + ownership + suite-green against the learner's working tree; `reset-lab`/`verify-lab` verified end-to-end. C6 lab-traced; areas 9,10 traced; `make check` green. Consumes verified `plan-mode`/`thinking` vd keys. Woven CV/security edge (foreign-project 404) satisfies R10.AC7 for this workflow lab.

- [x] **U6** `06-tdd` (C7, W2) — Daily-Driver **red→green** test-first loop. `unit.md` references generalized W2 in `meta/workflows.md`; teaches writing the test first, **confirming red for the right reason** (assertion, not import/typo), implementing to green, and **reading the impl** (a test can be satisfied the wrong way) — the CV gate for test-driven work. **Test-first lab** `u06-lab1` (write-path): add an `overdue` filter to `GET /tasks` (overdue iff `due_date` strictly before today **and** `status != done`; done/due-today/no-due-date excluded; absent param unchanged). Git refs `start/u06-lab1` (tag) + `solution/u06-lab1` (branch: `TaskFilters.overdue` + predicate in `list_tasks` vs `date.today()` + one route query param + edge-case tests); objective `verify.sh` checks contract + no-regression suite-green against the learner's working tree; `verify-lab` verified end-to-end (fails clean = all 6 returned, the right-reason red; passes on solution). C7 lab-traced; area 11 traced; `make check` green. Consumes `test-run` vd key (kept `unverified` under L1). Edge-case assertions are the woven CV (R10.AC7).

- [x] **U7** `07-debug-a-failure` (C8, W3) — Daily-Driver **scientific-method debugging**: reproduce → capture a re-runnable repro → **confirm root cause** (resist the AI's plausible symptom-patch) → fix → no-regress. `unit.md` references generalized W3 in `meta/workflows.md`. **Debug lab** `u07-lab1` on the **legacy** `taskflow-cli`: fix baked-in bug **D1** (overdue never flags — ISO date compared against `MM-DD-YYYY` string, copy-pasted in 3 sites) at its root. Git refs `start/u07-lab1` (tag = legacy with bug present) + `solution/u07-lab1` (branch: correct `is_overdue` via `date.fromisoformat`, `fmt_due`/`print_task_full` routed through it). Objective `verify.sh` drives the CLI via subprocess over a throwaway DB (legacy has no pytest): past-due flagged across `list --overdue`/`stats`/`list`+`show` display, future/no-due/done excluded, basic CLI intact; verified end-to-end (fails on start = the automated repro, passes on solution). C8 lab-traced; area 12 traced; `make check` green. No version-specific keys (no L1 debt). D2/D3 left for extra practice + U9.

+      - [x] **U8** `08-git-and-pr` (C9, W4) — Daily-Driver **turn work into a reviewable PR**: stage deliberately into atomic commits, messages that explain *why*, a PR description that matches `git diff main...HEAD`, self-review as the reviewer. `unit.md` references generalized W4 in `meta/workflows.md`; dogfooding worked example = this repo's own clean-commit/PR history. **Prose-self-check lab** `u08-lab1` (NO `start/`/`solution/` refs, NO `verify.sh` — like U2/U4): make a real `archived`-flag change in `taskflow-api` review-ready, graded by an objective reviewer's checklist (R7.AC3); optional BYO `gh pr create` stretch (R7.AC8, non-verifiable; `gh` not required per R7.AC7). C9 + area 13 lab-traced via front matter + `## Lab`; consumes **verified** `git-pr` key (no L1 debt). `make check` green. Not in `SEEDED.md` §2 (no defect/branch).

---
name: project-claude-code-mastery
description: The claude-training repo is a spec-driven course to take devs from AI-novice to elite Claude Code user
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 60da1c88-1049-436f-b19d-64dfecc96d3a
---

`/home/jj/dev/claude-training` is a **spec-driven** training course: "Claude Code Mastery." Goal: take a seasoned developer new to AI coding → elite Claude Code practitioner covering all advanced features + industry best-practice workflows. **Latest-targeting, not pinned** — the verified CLI version lives in `meta/version-record.md` (do NOT hardcode a version from memory; see [[feedback-version-currency-caveat]]).

Spec lives in `specs/claude-code-mastery/` using Kiro-style phases with approval gates: `requirements.md` (EARS) → `design.md` → `tasks.md`.

**Locked decisions (2026-05-29):**
- Deliverable: Markdown curriculum + runnable hands-on labs against a single sample codebase + reference solutions (self-contained repo).
- F1 — **Outcome-driven**: use cases (jobs-to-be-done) are the primary organizing axis, prioritized by frequency × leverage; the 17 features are a *secondary coverage guarantee* (matrix, all in ≥1 example).
- F2 — **Tiered depth for senior engineers**: assume experienced fast learners; per-unit fast-path/TL;DR; no programming/Git/terminal basics; core deep, niche awareness-level.
- F3 — **Version-resilient, NOT pinned**: target the current/latest CLI; principles-first body with version-specific details quarantined as `{{vd:key}}` in `meta/version-data.yaml`; the spec is the update instrument with a documented refresh process.
- Assessment: **capstone only** — one graded project; units instructional, labs ungraded practice; rubric covers the full ladder.
- Audience: **self-paced solo only** — NO facilitator/cohort/instructor materials.

**Key structural decisions from R-by-R review (2026-05-29):**
- R1 — progress tracked via **non-ranking capability map** of "can-do" statements grouped into neutral stages (First Wins → Daily Driver → Force Multiplier → Autonomy & Scale). NO novice/intermediate/elite labels in learner-facing text ("elite" is internal design target only).
- R7 — **two** sample codebases: primary + small deliberately-messy legacy repo (for onboarding/refactor/debug). Solutions as inspectable git artifacts.
- R10 — dedicated security unit + woven reinforcement; hands-on prompt-injection lab; verification defined as more than green tests.
- R12 — single machine-readable version-data file referenced by key; automated drift-detection; hard ban on memory-only version claims.
- R13 — required automated enforcement suite (front-matter validation, coverage/map cross-validation, traceability checks) run as local hooks + CI; doubles as worked examples.
- Resolved: Q3 (choose-your-own capstone brief + exemplar) and Q4 (macOS/Linux/WSL; native Windows out of v1). Still open for Design: Q1 (codebase stacks), Q2 (use-case catalog, capability map contents, course size).

**Spec files in `specs/claude-code-mastery/`:** `requirements.md` (✅ APPROVED, R1–R15, frozen — changes need a recorded decision), `design.md` (✅ APPROVED & merged to `main`), `tasks.md` (chunked index → `tasks/P1–P6*.md` per-section files), `decisions.md` (rationale/why + rejected alternatives + the canonical 🔓 open-loops ledger; survives session boundary), `IMPLEMENTATION.md` (**START HERE** every session: read order, **§3 = live phase status**, context protocol, build Definition of Done), `README.md` (nav hub).

**Resolved (no longer open):** Q1 domain = task/project tracker (`taskflow-api` primary + `taskflow-cli` legacy, both Python); Q2 = 16 content-driven units, capability map C1–C17 + CV. Q3/Q4 settled in session 1.

**Live status is `IMPLEMENTATION.md` §3 — trust it over this file.** As of 2026-05-30: P1–P4 ✅ done; **P5 (author the 16 units) in progress — U1–U7 ✅, U8–U16 remaining**; P6 (capstone/case-study/finalization) remaining. Work one unit per slice (context protocol); `make check` is the authoring gate (stays green), `make check-strict` is the v1-done gate (red until P6). Branch `spec/tasks-phase`; remote `origin` (gitea) — [[feedback-ask-before-push]]. Honor phase gates ([[feedback-spec-driven-honor-gates]]), keep §3 + the ledger current ([[feedback-continuity-hygiene]]), verify version-specifics vs installed CLI ([[feedback-version-currency-caveat]]).

content.content: Updated task #9 status

content.text:
**U8 (`08-git-and-pr`) is authored.** Committed `e823670` on `spec/tasks-phase`; `make check` green (C9 dropped out of the "not practiced by a lab" list).

**What shipped**
- **`course/units/08-git-and-pr/unit.md`** — core unit for C9 / W4: stage deliberately into atomic commits, write messages that explain *why*, open a PR whose description matches the diff, and self-review as the reviewer before requesting one. Recurring theme: "Claude drafts; you sign." Worked example dogfoods this repo's own clean-commit/solution-branch history.

**Design decisions worth flagging**
- **No `verify.sh`, no `start/`/`solution/` refs** — a deliberate choice, not a shortcut. Two reasons: (1) commit/PR *quality* is a judgment call, not mechanically checkable; (2) a real PR needs a remote + `gh`, which R7.AC7 forbids requiring — and `gh` isn't even installed here. So the lab follows the **U2/U4 precedent**: an objective **reviewer's-checklist** self-check (applying it *is* the W4 skill), plus an optional BYO `gh pr create` stretch marked non-verifiable (R7.AC8).
- The lab gives a concrete real change (`archived` flag on projects + `?include_archived=` filter) so the exercise isn't hand-wavy, but the rigor lives in the commit/PR craft and the checklist.
- Consumes the **already-verified** `git-pr` key — **no new L1 debt**. Not added to SEEDED §2 (no defect/branch, like U2/U4).

**State synced:** decisions (P5-U8-lab / P5-U8-vd + L7 note), IMPLEMENTATION §3, tasks.md, memory.

Nothing to push beyond the branch this time (no lab refs). Want me to **push `spec/tasks-phase`**? And next up is **U9 (`09-onboard-refactor-legacy`, C10, W5 + W8-deep)** — back on the messy `taskflow-cli`, which is a meatier one (multi-file refactor with the suite kept green, using the legacy smells + a `solution/u09-lab1` branch).

Should we start with a fresh context for that meatier work in the next unit?

content.text:
Honestly, not required for space — you're at 22% (778k free), so there's no compaction pressure and no quality cost to continuing here. But it's still a reasonable choice, for a different reason: U9 is a self-contained slice, and this context is now carrying a lot of U6/U7/U8 detail that U9 doesn't need. The continuity design is built for exactly this — IMPLEMENTATION §3, the decisions ledger, and memory are all current, so a fresh session can pick up U9 cleanly with the one-unit-per-slice load.

So: **your call, and a fresh context is a clean, well-supported option** — I'd lean toward it mainly for tidiness, not necessity.

One thing to settle first, either way: **U8's commit `e823670` is committed but not pushed** (no lab refs to push this time, but the branch is ahead of `origin`). If you're about to `/clear`, want me to push `spec/tasks-phase` now so origin matches your local before we switch?

A fresh U9 session would start by reading `IMPLEMENTATION.md` §3 → `decisions.md` (P5-U9 isn't written yet, but L7 + the legacy smells/D2–D3 in `SEEDED.md` §1 are) → the U9 row in `tasks/P5-units.md`, then load only `meta/workflows.md` (W5 + W8) and the legacy `taskflow-cli`.

Want me to **push, then you `/clear`** — or just continue here?
